# Portfolio Summary

## GitHub Repository Description

C++20 deterministic turn-based battle simulation demonstrating ECS-style gameplay architecture, feature packs, data-driven rules, trace tooling, CI checks, and scoped AI-agent workflows.

## LinkedIn Summary

Built a compact C++20 gamedev architecture sandbox: deterministic battle simulation, ECS-style runtime, feature-owned gameplay modules, config-driven feature packs, JSON-defined unit rules, scheduled engine loop, trace inspection, CTest/CI regression checks, and documented AI-agent development workflow with human-owned review gates.

## One-Minute Read

The project is intentionally small enough to review, but broad enough to demonstrate senior engineering judgment:

- clear module boundaries and dependency direction;
- feature-owned gameplay behavior instead of domain leakage into Core;
- deterministic scenario validation and golden-output tests;
- data-driven extension without turning JSON into scripting;
- trace tooling for observability;
- CI and architecture boundary checks;
- explicit AI-agent workflow with task packets, acceptance criteria, and review.
